meaning: n.留声机,电唱机vi.灌入留声机
examples: In the phonograph parlors, customers listened to recordings through individual ear tubes, moving from one machine to the next to hear different recorded speeches or pieces of music.
By the 1910s, much of the Brazilian first phonograph records are choros.
The rooftop's music is background music and Midge's apartment music is coming from the phonograph .
Barraud captured Nipper, listening intently to an Edison phonograph, in his 1898 painting His master's Voice.
